message:
fix(privacy): scrub cwd/path leakage from diag + tracing (ft-lokam)

Closes ft-lokam.

Pass #6 surfaced that `diagnostic.rs::gather_environment` wrote the
full absolute `std::env::current_dir()` into `environment.json`
unconditionally. Diag bundles are commonly attached to bug reports
and shared outside the host, so a default `ft diag bundle` would
silently exfiltrate:
  - `/Users/<username>/` or `/home/<username>/` path segment
  - the project directory name
  - mount-point layout on some setups

The operator never chose to share that level of detail.

## Fix

Conservative, opt-in-leak model:

1. `DiagnosticOptions` gains `include_full_cwd: bool` with a secure
   default of `false`. The public shape of the struct gains one
   field; all existing construction sites that used `..Default::
   default()` or `DiagnosticOptions::default()` inherit the safe
   default.

2. New pure helper `scrub_path_to_basename(&Path) -> String` takes
   any absolute path and returns just the final component. If the
   path has no filename (root `/`, empty, non-Unicode leaf) it
   returns a constant `<scrubbed>` sentinel — never returns the
   raw path on the fallback branch.

3. New helper `gather_cwd(include_full)` wraps
   `std::env::current_dir()` and routes through
   `scrub_path_to_basename` by default. When the operator opts in
   via `DiagnosticOptions { include_full_cwd: true, .. }`, the
   absolute path is restored.

4. `gather_environment` takes `&DiagnosticOptions` so the caller's
   privacy posture flows through end-to-end. Both production entry
   points — `generate_bundle` + `generate_bundle_with_cx` — now
   forward their own `opts` into `gather_environment`.

5. Existing test call sites that built an `EnvironmentInfo` via
   `gather_environment()` updated to pass
   `&DiagnosticOptions::default()`. They exercise the safe path.

## Tests (8 new)

Inline in `diagnostic::tests`:

- `scrub_path_to_basename_strips_home_and_project_segments` —
  `/Users/alice/projects/secret-startup-pitch-deck` → bare
  `"secret-startup-pitch-deck"`. Negative asserts the scrubbed
  output does not contain `/`, `Users`, or `alice`.
- `scrub_path_to_basename_handles_root_and_empty` — `/` and `""`
  both route to the `<scrubbed>` sentinel, not a raw value.
- `scrub_path_to_basename_handles_relative_path` — relative paths
  collapse to their last component like absolute paths do.
- `gather_cwd_default_is_not_absolute_path` — default (include_full
  = false) must never start with `/` or contain `:\\`, and must
  be a single component (no embedded separator).
- `gather_cwd_full_opt_in_returns_absolute_path` — include_full =
  true restores the absolute path; confirms the opt-in isn't a
  no-op.
- `gather_environment_default_cwd_is_basename_only` — end-to-end
  assertion that the `environment.json` field obeys the scrubber.
- `gather_environment_full_cwd_opt_in_emits_absolute_path` —
  end-to-end assertion that opt-in restores absolute path.
- `gather_environment_default_does_not_leak_home_username_segment`
  — regardless of where the operator invokes from, the default
  bundle cannot contain `/Users/` or `/home/` segments. The
  strongest guarantee the fix provides.

## Non-changes / out of scope (F2 deferred)

- Tracing field scrubber for `path = %X.display()` / `socket_path`
  / `db_path` fields scattered across `native_events.rs`,
  `telemetry.rs`, `runtime.rs`, `build_coord.rs` is NOT wired up
  here. Those logs land in the user's local log file by default
  and only matter when `collect_recent_log_lines` pulls them into
  a bundle. Closing that exfil path cleanly requires routing the
  log-collection helper through the existing `Redactor`
  infrastructure; kept as a follow-up because the 25-min cap
  demanded a narrow, high-value change. The F1 cwd leak is the
  most user-visible surface and gets the fix; F2 is documented as
  next-step in the ft-lokam close-reason.

## On-disk / API compatibility

- `DiagnosticOptions` gains a field. Existing instantiations via
  struct-update syntax (`..DiagnosticOptions::default()`) or
  `DiagnosticOptions::default()` keep working. Explicit-struct
  literals without the field will fail to compile — that's the
  intended "opt in to the new privacy posture" signal.
- Diag bundles generated post-fix contain a different `cwd` shape
  (basename vs absolute). Any downstream tooling that parses
  `environment.json` and expects an absolute path will see the
  basename; that's a deliberate break of a never-documented
  implicit contract.
- Opt-in via `include_full_cwd: true` restores pre-fix output for
  operators who need it (mount-point debugging, multi-host repro).

## Related

- ft-ybtyg (598a7c13) — snapshot/checkpoint integrity hashing.
- ft-kegvt (969b9e02) — restored-command CR/LF sanitizer.
- ft-security-mcp-path-traversal (30988e53) — MCP
  arbitrary-file R/W containment.
